architecture.summary: CCM-LoRA augments standard LoRA with a learned lightweight router that dynamically selects which LoRA rank directions to activate per input token. The router produces input-dependent masks over the LoRA rank space, enabling sparsity in both fine-tuning and inference. The system is trained with a budget-constrained objective that targets an expected effective rank or FLOPs while regularizing routing patterns to prevent degenerate always-on or always-off behaviors.

biblio.title: Context-Conditioned Masked LoRA: Dynamic Rank Routing for Compute-Efficient Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning

claims.statement: CCM-LoRA learns a lightweight router that activates an input-dependent subset of LoRA rank directions, turning LoRA into dynamic rank routing and enabling contextual sparsity in fine-tuning and inference
CCM-LoRA is trained with a budget-constrained objective that targets an expected effective rank (or FLOPs) while regularizing routing to avoid degenerate always-on/off masks
Across public NLU and multilingual benchmarks, CCM-LoRA improves the accuracy-efficiency Pareto frontier versus static-rank LoRA and adaptive-rank baselines
CCM-LoRA matches or improves task performance at lower inference-time effective rank
The paper provides a reproducible profiling protocol and analyses of rank usage, router overhead, and robustness under domain and language shift
